machine learning.title: Causal Connections: Leveraging Multilingual Fine-Tuning for Financial QA@FinCausal 2026
machine learning.summary: The paper compares encoder‑only token tagging with multilingual BERT, encoder‑decoder generation with multilingual BART, and decoder‑only LLMs such as Llama 3.1 and GPT variants. Prompting and few‑shot examples achieved competitive performance, while supervised fine‑tuning gave the largest gains. GPT‑4.1 Mini fine‑tuned on combined English and Spanish data tied for the highest score on the English subtask (4.8140) and ranked third on the Spanish subtask (4.7753).

machine learning.title: NVIDIA Doubles Down on DLSS 4.5 With Smarter Ray Reconstruction at Computex, But DLSS 5 Is a No-Show - Wccftech
machine learning.summary: NVIDIA unveiled DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction, a second‑generation transformer model that promises to improve ray traced and path traced image quality across supported PC games. DLSS 4.5 Ray Reconstruction will be available in August. The new model delivers 35% more compute capability and processes 20% more parameters while maintaining similar performance to the previous model. The model offers developers greater control over temporal accumulation, enabling precise tuning of model response for better image quality.

machine learning.title: PSK@EEUCA 2026: Fine-Tuning Large Language Models with Synthetic Data Augmentation for Multi-Class Toxicity Detection in Gaming Chat
machine learning.summary: The best system combined Llama 3.1 8B with 5 % synthetic data augmentation. This system achieved an F1‑macro score of 0.6234 on the test set, placing fourth out of 35 teams in the EEUCA 2026 Toxicity Detection task. The authors observed a “validation trap” where high validation performance correlated with poor test transfer.

machine learning.title: BITS Pilani at SemEval-2026 Task 9: Structured Supervised Fine-Tuning with DPO Refinement for Polarization Detection
machine learning.summary: The team fine‑tuned Qwen 2.5‑7B‑Instruct with LoRA using an interpretable slot‑filling template and then applied Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) with automatically generated preference pairs. Their submitted system reached 0.7664 Macro‑F1 on the English test set, and post‑submission experiments with Mistral‑Nemo‑Instruct‑2407 and LLM‑judge‑filtered pairs improved performance to 0.8162 Macro‑F1. This performance surpassed the organiser baseline of 0.7802.

machine learning.title: Dual swin transformer for assisting in the diagnosis and surgical prediction of necrotizing enterocolitis - Nature
machine learning.summary: The study developed a dual Swin Transformer integrating abdominal X‑rays (2D branch) and laboratory parameters (1D branch) via late fusion. The optimized multimodal model achieved an AUC of 0.915 for NEC diagnosis on the internal test set. On the independent external test set, the model achieved an AUC of 0.903 for NEC diagnosis. Interpretability analyses highlighted intestinal pneumatosis and C‑reactive protein as key predictive factors.

machine learning.title: NTIRE 2026 Rip Current Detection and Segmentation (RipDetSeg) Challenge Report
machine learning.summary: The NTIRE 2026 Rip Current Detection and Segmentation Challenge attracted 159 registered participants and received 9 valid test submissions across detection and segmentation tasks. Most participant solutions relied on pretrained models combined with strong augmentation and post‑processing designs. The results indicate that rip‑current understanding benefits strongly from the progress of robust general‑purpose vision models.

machine learning.title: SelfMatch: Combining Contrastive Self-Supervision and Consistency for Semi-Supervised Learning
machine learning.summary: SelfMatch combines contrastive self-supervised pretraining with augmentation consistency regularization for semi-supervised learning. On CIFAR-10 with 40 labeled examples, SelfMatch achieves 93.19% accuracy, outperforming MixMatch (52.46%), UDA (70.95%), ReMixMatch (80.9%), and FixMatch (86.19%). The method narrows the gap between supervised learning (95.87%) and semi-supervised learning (93.19%) using only a few labels per class.

machine learning.title: Self-supervised multimodal transformer for fine-grained detection of controlled perturbation events in piano performance - Nature
machine learning.summary: The paper proposes a self‑supervised multimodal Transformer framework that fuses audio spectral features, symbolic MIDI representations, and a MIDI‑derived spatial/kinematic proxy. The two‑stage training strategy uses contrastive learning, masked prediction, and temporal reconstruction objectives during pre‑training. Fine‑tuning optimizes detection of five error categories—pitch, timing, dynamics, touch, and pedal—in piano performance. Experiments on the public MAESTRO dataset show that multimodal fusion outperforms unimodal approaches.

machine learning.title: Generalizable self-supervised learning for imaging flow cytometry on multi-dataset leukocyte differential - Nature
machine learning.summary: The authors propose a self‑supervised contrastive learning framework pretrained on a non‑annotated dataset of bright‑field leukocyte images. When frozen and combined with lightweight linear or multilayer perceptron heads, the method achieves classification accuracies of 96.16%, 96.68%, and 92.24% on three independent leukocyte datasets. The performance is comparable to supervised baselines despite using no annotated training data.

machine learning.title: Elastic Weight Consolidation Improves the Robustness of Self-Supervised Learning Methods under Transfer
machine learning.summary: The work re-interprets SSL fine-tuning through Bayesian continual learning and applies Elastic Weight Consolidation regularization. Using EWC with SSL improves worst sub-group performance by 5% on Waterbirds and 2% on Celeb-A when using the ViT-B/16 architecture. The authors pre-compute and publicly release the Fisher Information Matrix evaluated with 10,000 ImageNet-1K variates on modern SSL architectures including ViT-B/16 and ResNet50 trained with DINO.

machine learning.title: MEMe: An Accurate Maximum Entropy Method for Efficient Approximations in Large-Scale Machine Learning
machine learning.summary: MEMe is a novel robust maximum entropy algorithm capable of handling hundreds of moments for computationally efficient approximations. The method is equivalent to constrained Bayesian variational inference. MEMe outperforms existing approaches in fast log determinant estimation and information-theoretic Bayesian optimisation.

machine learning.title: Kirkland Hints It Could Fine-Tune LLMs For Own Legal AI Model - Artificial Lawyer
machine learning.summary: Kirkland & Ellis’s $500 million technology project could involve fine‑tuning open‑source large language models to create their own legal AI model. Two new AI Infrastructure Director positions at Kirkland require experience with on‑premise GPU environments. The job adverts mention experience with existing legal AI platforms such as Harvey, Legora, CoCounsel, and Lexis+ AI.

machine learning.title: Snowflake and Anthropic Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption Driven by Rising Demand for Governed AI - Financial Times
machine learning.summary: Snowflake Cortex AI adds governance, security, observability, and scale to Anthropic models that run directly on data within Snowflake. Snowflake and Anthropic cite enterprise customers such as Basis, Block, Carvana, eSentire, Indeed, and Notion. The partnership aims to accelerate enterprise AI adoption driven by rising demand for governed AI.

machine learning.title: Self-Supervised Learning for Large-Scale Unsupervised Image Clustering
machine learning.summary: The paper proposes a simple scheme for unsupervised classification based on self-supervised representations. It achieves 39% accuracy on ImageNet with 1000 clusters and 46% accuracy with overclustering. The authors suggest adding the unsupervised evaluation to a set of standard benchmarks for self-supervised learning. The code for the method is available at https://github.com/Randl/kmeans_selfsuper.

machine learning.title: MerLin: A Discovery Engine for Photonic and Hybrid Quantum Machine Learning
machine learning.summary: MerLin is an open-source framework that integrates optimized strong simulation of linear optical circuits into PyTorch and scikit-learn workflows for differentiable training of quantum layers. The framework reproduces eighteen state-of-the-art photonic and hybrid quantum machine learning works spanning kernel methods, reservoir computing, convolutional and recurrent architectures, generative models, and modern training paradigms. MerLin enables systematic benchmarking, reproducibility, and hardware-aware testing on available quantum hardware while allowing exploration beyond current capabilities.

machine learning.title: Sensitive Enterprise Data Uploads to AI Models Double in a Year - Infosecurity Magazine
machine learning.summary: The Zscaler 2026 AI Threat Report found a 93% year‑over‑year increase in employees transferring enterprise data to AI and machine learning applications. Employees transferred a total of 18,033 TB of data to AI tools in the last year, equivalent to about 3.6 billion digital photos. Over half of the data transfers were driven by Grammarly (38%) and ChatGPT (21%), and Zscaler identified 410 million DLP violations related to ChatGPT.

machine learning.title: Changing Data Sources in the Age of Machine Learning for Official Statistics
machine learning.summary: The paper outlines the main risks, liabilities, and uncertainties associated with changing data sources in machine learning for official statistics. It provides a checklist of prevalent origins and causes of changing data sources, covering technical, ownership, ethics, regulation, and public perception aspects. Repercussions highlighted include concept drift, bias, availability, validity, accuracy, completeness, neutrality, and potential discontinuation of statistical offerings. Precautionary measures recommended include enhancing robustness in data sourcing and statistical techniques and conducting thorough monitoring.
